title: Choking (Infant)

steps.title: Check if they can cry or cough
steps.instruction: If the infant can cry, cough, or breathe — let them keep coughing. Do NOT intervene yet. Monitoring carefully is key. If they stop making noise, act immediately.

steps.title: Position for back blows
steps.instruction: Place the infant facedown on your forearm, resting on your thigh. Support their head by holding the jaw (not neck). Keep the head lower than the chest.

steps.title: Give 5 back blows
steps.instruction: Give 5 firm, distinct back blows between the shoulder blades using the heel of your hand. Gravity helps the object drop out.

steps.title: Position for chest thrusts
steps.instruction: Sandwich the infant between your forearms and turn them face-up. Support the back of the head. Keep the head lower than the chest on your lap/thigh.

steps.title: Give 5 chest thrusts
steps.instruction: Give 5 quick, firm chest thrusts in the center of the chest (just below the nipple line) using the heel of one hand. Allow the chest to recoil between thrusts.

steps.title: Alternate and repeat
steps.instruction: Keep alternating 5 back blows and 5 chest thrusts until the object is forced out, the infant starts to cry/breathe, or they become unresponsive.

steps.title: If they become unresponsive
steps.instruction: Stop and lower them to a flat, firm surface. Call 101 immediately if not already done. Begin 30:2 CPR. Check the mouth for the object during breaths — only remove if seen. NO blind finger sweeps.

warnings: Do NOT perform abdominal thrusts (Heimlich maneuver) on infants
Do NOT do blind finger sweeps — it can push objects deeper
Do NOT hang the infant by their feet
Do NOT squeeze the throat or neck while supporting the head
sources: Magen David Adom Infant First Aid Protocols
